data.title: RED: Write behavioral tests for sprint-review-scope-check.sh
data.description:
## Description
Create behavioral test file for the new sprint-review-scope-check.sh script that detects when code review findings reference files outside the original task file impact table.

## TDD Requirement
Write failing tests FIRST. Tests will fail because the script does not exist yet. Add RED markers to .test-index.

## Test Approach
Create mock reviewer-findings.json files and mock ticket CLI returning task descriptions with ## File Impact sections. Execute sprint-review-scope-check.sh with these fixtures and assert on structured output (OUT_OF_SCOPE vs IN_SCOPE) and exit codes.

## Test Scenarios
1. All findings reference files within task scope → IN_SCOPE, exit 0
2. Finding references file not in task file impact → OUT_OF_SCOPE: <files>, exit 0
3. No reviewer-findings.json found → graceful skip (IN_SCOPE), exit 0
4. Task has no file impact section → graceful skip, exit 0
5. Missing arguments → non-zero exit with usage

## Files to Create
- tests/scripts/test-sprint-review-scope-check.sh

## Files to Modify
- .test-index (add mapping with RED markers)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/scripts/test-sprint-review-scope-check.sh
- [ ] Test file contains at least 4 test sections
  Verify: grep -c 'echo "Test [0-9]' tests/scripts/test-sprint-review-scope-check.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 4)}'
- [ ] .test-index entry maps source to test with RED markers
  Verify: grep -q 'sprint-review-scope-check' .test-index
- [ ] Tests are behavioral: create mock findings and ticket data, execute script, assert on output
  Verify: grep -q 'reviewer.*findings\|findings.*json' tests/scripts/test-sprint-review-scope-check.sh
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (RED markers tolerate failures)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
